rulings.Case Title: DAVID MELANSON VS. S.J. AMOROSO, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 3, DEFENDANT S.J. AMOROSO, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Is granted. The hirer of an independent contractor is not liable to an employee of the contractor merely because the hirer retained control over safety conditions at a workcite, but a hirer is liable to an employee of a contractor insofar as a hirer's exercise of retained control affirmatively contributed to the employee's injuries. Hooker vs. Dept. of Transportation (2002) 29 Cal.4th 198, 202. Defendant shifts the burden by showing defendant did not affirmatively contribute to the jury. No evidence presented that defendant took action that contributated to the injury. Request for continuance denied, Declaration not sufficient. CCP 437c(h). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID W. LING VS. CHRISTINE D. HAY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT CHRISTINE HAY MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Is denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendant was served with the deed in the Declaration of Disclosures and thus received adequate notice. Proof of service for the Declaration was filed with the Court. The substance of the notice requirement of F.C. sec. 2030(b)(3) was adequately fulfilled. Furthermore, F.C. sec. 2040 does not appear to mandate the harsh result of voiding the severance. The 7/28/2008 deed adequately described the subject property in compliance with CC sec. 1092. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 5, PLAINTIFF CORE-MARK INTERNATIONAL, INC., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Off Calendar, as moot. Court granted motion on 5/18/2010. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: 560 HAIGHT STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION INC A VS. 560 HAIGHT STREET, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 6, DEFENDANTS GEORGE BIRMINGHAM, THE BIRMINGHAM GROUP, INC. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 9th cause of action to plead facts about the specific nature of the fraud. No facts alleged as to what specifically was concealed, by whom, and under what duty. Plaintiff must plead facts as to knowledge of the defects, intent to conceal, and when, and by whom concealing representations were made. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 10th cause of action because willful misconduct is not a cause of action. Plaintiff can recover punitive damages from their other intentional tort and related causes of action. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN M THOMPSON VS. BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY, BANKERS INSURANCE GROUP, BANKERS FINANCIAL CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Is granted, 10 days leave to amend to plead punitive damages with greater specificity. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAWN M THOMPSON VS. BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANTS BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY, BANKERS INSURANCE GROUP, BANKERS FINANCIAL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Although a copy of the contract need not be attached to the complaint, plaintiff must plead the essential terms of the contract. Also, unclear whether one or more contracts are involved. Assignment of rights allegation is sufficiently pled. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RICHARD A. BREITBARTH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Post Settlement And Closure Of The Account And Affidavit Of Payment Or Deposit Into Registry
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, May 28, 2010, line 9, PETITIONERS RICHARD BREITBARTH, MARIA BREITBARTH, Motion for Post Settlement and Closure of the Account and Affidavit of Payment or Deposit into Registry. Motion denied. Insufficient showing that plaintiffs are entitled to requested relief. Motion for injunction not calendared. Furthermore, plaintiffs fail to demonstrate a reasonable probability of success on the merits. =(302/CWW)
